Class Pedestrian

java.lang.Object
greenfoot.Actor
SuperSmoothMover
Pedestrian
Direct Known Subclasses:
Civilian, MineDropper
public abstract class Pedestrian extends SuperSmoothMover
A Pedestrian that tries to walk across the street. Pedestrians move vertically across lanes, avoiding vehicles when possible. They can be knocked down by vehicles and later healed.

NOTE: This class is designed to be converted into a superclass for different types of pedestrians (e.g., Person, Animal, etc.) as part of the student assignment.

Version:
2023
Author:
Jordan Cohen

      public void act()
      Act - do whatever the Pedestrian wants to do. This method is called whenever the 'Act' or 'Run' button gets pressed in the environment.

    • canMoveTo

      protected boolean canMoveTo(int nextX, int nextY)
      Codex change: allow movement only if the next position does not overlap a blocked pixel in the background collision mask.
      Parameters:
      nextX - the candidate x-coordinate for the pedestrian center
      nextY - the candidate y-coordinate for the pedestrian center
      Returns:
      true if the movement is allowed
